parentQuestion: The trochlear nerve (CN IV) is unique because it ___.

scaffolds.q: Most cranial nerves exit from the front (anterior/ventral) surface of the brainstem.
scaffolds.explain: Cranial nerves typically emerge from the anterior surface of the brainstem like cables coming out of the front of a router. Let me show you the exception — CN IV sneaks out the back door. Elegant deviation from the pattern.

scaffolds.q: CN IV controls the superior oblique muscle of the eye.
scaffolds.explain: The trochlear nerve (CN IV) innervates only one muscle: the superior oblique, which helps rotate and depress the eye. Invite this in:this — specialized control for a specialized job.
